reasoning_model_1: The audio content ('Are you sure? You really didn't touch my things?') fully matches the target script word-for-word ('Are you sure you really didn't touch my things?'). The splitting of the sentence into two prosodic units ('Are you sure?' This phrasing creates a dramatic, interrogative pause that mimics a person scrutinizing another for a lie, effectively conveying suspicion and doubt. The delivery is judged as fluent and the paralinguistic expression is vivid and accurate.
reasoning_model_2: The generated speech for Model 2 is identical to Model 1 in both textual content and prosodic structure representation. The content accurately reflects the script. The paralinguistic features align perfectly with the 'Cognitive State' requirement, delivering the line with the appropriate interrogative and suspicious nuance.
model_comparison: Both models produced audio representations that are identical in content and prosodic structure. Both successfully integrated the target text with the requested paralinguistic feature (doubting tone/Cognitive State). The choice to break the sentence into two distinct questions ('Are you sure?' and 'You really didn't touch my things?') enhances the emotional delivery of doubt.
